programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet
programme.desc: Over millions of years, plants animals, even humans have had to adapt to an Evolving Earth. Travel 1350 ft into the Earth. Follow Dinosaur tracks. Gain insight into our planet's past and reveal how much our landscape has changed.

programme.title: Xploration Awesome Planet
programme.desc: The oceans cover 71 percent of our planet and contain countless species, but what's most incredible is that human beings have explored less than 5 percent of the oceans, leading one to wonder what kinds of incredible discoveries are yet to be made.

programme.title: Xploration: Animal Science
programme.desc: In this episode we meet human's closest relatives - primates. See what makes a chimpanzee one of the most intelligent primates. Find out why the spider monkey is such an amazing climber and feel the power of the howler monkey's howl.

programme.title: Xploration: Animal Science
programme.desc: In this episode we'll find out how sharks use electro-sensors to detect their prey. Also, discover the skunk's stinky defense mechanism. And domestic versus wild - see how the striped zebra matches up against its cousin the horse.

programme.title: Super Bowl Classic Games
programme.desc: Relive the Super Bowl XXII matchup between the Denver Broncos and the Washington Redskins. After trailing 10-0 at the end of the first quarter of Super Bowl XXII, the Redskins scored 42 unanswered points, including a record-breaking 35 points in the second quarter, and setting several other Super Bowl records.

programme.title: A Football Life
programme.desc: Doug Williams grew up without running water in rural Louisiana, played quarterback in an era when few African Americans did, overcoming prejudice and hardship to become a Heisman candidate, a first round draft pick, and ultimately, a Super Bowl MVP.

programme.title: NFL Game Re-Airs
programme.desc: Leigh Bodden had three of the four interceptions thrown by Mark Sanchez and the New England Patriots defense allowed just one touchdown and 226 yards in a 31-14 win over the Jets.

programme.title: NFL Game Re-Airs
programme.desc: Manning threw for 308 yards and four touchdowns, including a 65-yarder to Reggie Wayne to go ahead for good, and the Colts beat the Jacksonville Jaguars 35-31 Thursday night to improve to 14-0 for the first time in franchise history.

programme.title: NFL Game Re-Airs
programme.desc: Drew Brees torched the Giants' league-leading defense, Saints defenders ruined Eli Manning's homecoming and Saints beat the Giants 48-27.

programme.title: NFL Game Re-Airs
programme.desc: Derrick Henry surpassed 2,000 yards rushing and rookie Sam Sloman's 37-yard field goal that bounced off the upright and through for a 41-38 victory over the Houston Texans.

programme.title: NFL Game Re-Airs
programme.desc: Watch the matchup between the Minnesota Vikings and Los Angeles Rams where Jared Goff passed for career highs of 465 yards and five touchdowns, winning a scintillating duel with his Minnesota counterpart and leading unbeaten Los Angeles to a 38-31 victory Thursday night.

programme.title: Hard Knocks: Training Camp With the Atlanta Falcons
programme.desc: Head coach Mike Smith and a roster of 90 players begin training camp in Flowery Branch, Ga.; the team goes through the first week of on-field drills in preparation for the upcoming season.

programme.title: Hard Knocks: Training Camp With the Atlanta Falcons
programme.desc: General manager Thomas Dimitroff discusses the 2014 roster; profile of linebacker Sean Weatherspoon; the Falcons scrimmage with the Tennessee Titans; the Falcons play their opening preseason game against the Miami Dolphins.

programme.title: Hard Knocks: Training Camp With the Atlanta Falcons
programme.desc: Training camp heats up with jobs on the line; the Falcons take on the Texans in two mid-week scrimmages; the team plays their second preseason game; competition for the backup quarterback job intensifies.

programme.title: Hard Knocks: Training Camp With the Atlanta Falcons
programme.desc: Fifteen players are cut from the team; veteran players prepare for the regular season; a thunderstorm sends the team heading for cover.

programme.title: Hard Knocks: Training Camp With the Atlanta Falcons
programme.desc: The Falcons travel to Jacksonville to take on the Jaguars; coaches evaluate player performances as they prepare to make cuts to finalize the 53-man roster.

programme.title: NFL 360
programme.desc: Raiders linebacker Carl Nassib comes out as gay and helps transform lives, and Super Bowl champion Osi Umenyiora helps Nigerian prospects escape dramatic hardships.

programme.title: NFL Films Presents
programme.desc: Jeff Rohrer, Esera Tuaolo, and Vince Lombardi are LGBTQ+ allys in sports, advocating for visibility and acceptance. Rohrer made history as the first openly gay NFL player, while Tuaolo courageously came out after retiring. Lombardi's legacy extends beyond football, fostering a culture of inclusivity. Celebrate their contributions during Pride Month.

programme.title: NFL Media Originals
programme.desc: This Pride Month, the NFL honors Richard Fierro for his acts of bravery as he and other survivors of the share their stories of healing, the importance of safe spaces and their hope for unity in the futures.

programme.desc: Countdown the top 60 Super Bowl plays in NFL history.

programme.desc: Brett Kollmann breaks down how the Butt Fumble was caused by events that took place 36 years prior.

programme.title: NFL Game Re-Airs
programme.desc: Adrian Peterson and Calvin Johnson duel for the most yards, but Peterson raced for the game-sealing touchdown midway through the fourth quarter, padding his NFL rushing lead with 171 yards on 27 carries for the Vikings in a 34-24 victory over the Lions.

programme.title: NFL Game Re-Airs
programme.desc: Drew Brees torched the Giants' league-leading defense, Saints defenders ruined Eli Manning's homecoming and Saints beat the Giants 48-27.

programme.desc: Johns Hopkins & Clemson: Explore two top-tier lacrosse programs as TLN takes you inside their world-class facilities. Then, head to Clemson, SC, for an exclusive look at the nation's premier women's lacrosse facilities.

programme.title: Through X
programme.desc: Joey Spallina - Paul Carcaterra visits Syracuse, N.Y. to pull back the curtain on the biggest name in College Lacrosse: Joey Spallina. In this episode of THROUGH X, Spallina speaks on wearing the No. 22 at Syracuse, shares his goal to return the Orange to NCAA Championship glory, and shows you how to score his signature behind-the-back goal.

programme.title: Mitchell Pehlke Lacrosse Show
programme.desc: On this special Father's Day Episode of the Mitchell Pehlke Lacrosse Show presented by Duke Cannon, we interviewed four famous lacrosse dads. Joe Spallina describes what it's like to raise potentially five D1 lacrosse players, Paul Carcaterra shares the trick that helped his daughter Peyla became one of the top girls lacrosse players in the country, Greg Kenneally (ECD Greg) details one invention idea to make parenting easier, and Matt Hasselbeck explains why so many NFL quarterbacks become Lacrosse Dads.

With a National title, the Tewaaraton Award winner, and record shattering season – she has cemented herself as an all-time great. We also went to Charlottesville, VA to dissect the elite game of Connor Shellenberger: a hometown hero, and arguably the best player in history for the University of Virginia.

programme.title: Brennan O’Neill Opens Up About Becoming ‘The Guy’

programme.title: Marcus Holman and the Moment He Became Obsessed with Lacrosse
programme.desc: Kyle Harrison sits down with Marcus Holman - one of lacrosse’s greatest shooters and leaders - for an honest conversation about family, obsession, adversity, longevity, and chasing the all-time goals record. Marcus takes us back to his earliest memories from John's Hopkins’ locker room to the moment he fell in love with shooting He opens up about evolving his role, becoming a leader, sustaining greatness into his mid-30s, and what it would mean to cement his legacy in the record books.

programme.desc: Joey Spallina - Paul Carcaterra visits Syracuse, N.Y. to pull back the curtain on the biggest name in College Lacrosse: Joey Spallina. In this episode of THROUGH X, Spallina speaks on wearing the No. 22 at Syracuse, shares his goal to return the Orange to NCAA Championship glory, and shows you how to score his signature behind-the-back goal.

programme.title: Mitchell Pehlke Lacrosse Show
programme.desc: On this special Father's Day Episode of the Mitchell Pehlke Lacrosse Show presented by Duke Cannon, we interviewed four famous lacrosse dads. Joe Spallina describes what it's like to raise potentially five D1 lacrosse players, Paul Carcaterra shares the trick that helped his daughter Peyla became one of the top girls lacrosse players in the country, Greg Kenneally (ECD Greg) details one invention idea to make parenting easier, and Matt Hasselbeck explains why so many NFL quarterbacks become Lacrosse Dads.

programme.title: Pregame Plates presented by Whirlpool
programme.desc: Jerry Ragonese, a 13-year pro lacrosse vet turned elite chef is taking his talents, and his long-time friends, into the kitchen. Cooking up epic dishes while uncovering the stories behind the meals that made the lacrosse stars of today.

programme.title: Through X
programme.desc: Paul Carcaterra heads to Evanston, IL to visit with Northwestern's Izzy Scane. Izzy has been a dominant force for the Wildcats since she first arrived on campus. With a National title, the Tewaaraton Award winner, and record shattering season – she has cemented herself as an all-time great. We also went to Charlottesville, VA to dissect the elite game of Connor Shellenberger: a hometown hero, and arguably the best player in history for the University of Virginia.

programme.title: The BEST Moments From Street Lacrosse in Atlanta

programme.title: Marcus Holman and the Moment He Became Obsessed with Lacrosse
programme.desc: Kyle Harrison sits down with Marcus Holman - one of lacrosse’s greatest shooters and leaders - for an honest conversation about family, obsession, adversity, longevity, and chasing the all-time goals record. Marcus takes us back to his earliest memories from John's Hopkins’ locker room to the moment he fell in love with shooting He opens up about evolving his role, becoming a leader, sustaining greatness into his mid-30s, and what it would mean to cement his legacy in the record books.

programme.desc: After one decision destroys the life of a young couple, it continues to ruin their other relationships and everything they hold sacred.

programme.title: Basquiat
programme.desc: Profile of artist Jean-Michel Basquiat.

programme.title: One Love
programme.desc: A rasta musician meets and falls for a gospel singer when they both enter a music contest in Kingston, Jamaica. However, her father has other ideas.

programme.title: Gang of Roses
programme.desc: The fierce head of an all-female former gang that broke up gets them in the saddle again to hunt down treasure and avenge her sister’s death.

programme.title: Nine Lives
programme.desc: A case of mistaken identity thrusts a former Special Forces agent suffering from PTSD back into the world of treachery he has tried to leave behind.

programme.title: False Witness
programme.desc: As their conflicting ideas begin to take a toll on their relationship, an investigator plans to prove his lover, an assistant DA, wrong about a case.

programme.title: Uncle P
programme.desc: Multimillionaire hip-hop superstar Master P is thrown headfirst into fatherhood in the suburbs when he becomes the guardian of his sister’s children.

programme.title: Good Fences
programme.desc: In the 1970s, an African-American attorney moves his family into the posh, white world of Greenwich, Connecticut—with provocative results.

programme.title: The Temptations
programme.desc: The true story behind the legendary Motown musical sensation is brought to life in this touching miniseries.

programme.title: The Best Thing!
programme.desc: A restaurateur discovers his deceased ex-girlfriend may have had his child.

programme.title: The Harder They Come
programme.desc: A talented young Jamaican man just wants to be a reggae singer, but finds himself dealing with corrupt record producers and menacing drug pushers.

programme.title: The Autobiography of Miss Jane Pittman
programme.desc: At 110 years old, a Louisiana woman born into slavery recounts her experience from the Civil War through her involvement in the civil rights movement.

programme.title: Uncle P
programme.desc: Multimillionaire hip-hop superstar Master P is thrown headfirst into fatherhood in the suburbs when he becomes the guardian of his sister’s children.

programme.title: Good Fences
programme.desc: In the 1970s, an African-American attorney moves his family into the posh, white world of Greenwich, Connecticut—with provocative results.

programme.title: Life of a King
programme.desc: A former felon looking for redemption tries to steer inner-city kids away from crime by forming an unconventional chess club in Washington, DC.

programme.title: Subspiral
programme.desc: A young man who has worked to take a different path from his wayward brother falls for a woman who forces him into the life he’d hoped to avoid.

programme.title: Hopetown
programme.desc: An ex Golden Glove champion attempts to fix his relationship with his sister.

programme.title: Stock Option
programme.desc: Sparks fly between a homeless stockbroker and a famous model after the man saves her from a robbery attempt.

programme.title: The Harder They Come
programme.desc: A talented young Jamaican man just wants to be a reggae singer, but finds himself dealing with corrupt record producers and menacing drug pushers.

programme.title: Don't Tell Mom the Babysitter's Dead
programme.desc: 17-year-old Tanya and her three siblings are left in the charge of a crotchety old babysitter who suddenly dies. Posing as an adult, Tanya gets an assistant gig at a fashion company and is thrust into the world of adulthood and parenting.

programme.title: Don't Shoot
programme.desc: Following the shooting of an unarmed Black teen by two White officers, a city is divided in the fight to seek social justice and reform.

programme.title: Fifty Shades of Black
programme.desc: An impressionable college student is introduced to the kinky world and dom fetish of entrepreneur Christian Black in this Fifty Shades of Grey parody.

programme.title: The Aftermath
programme.desc: After the new guy botches a perfectly planned armored car heist, four crafty criminals are left trapped, with the police closing in on them.

programme.title: Liquid Dreams
programme.desc: The Dawkins brothers, navigating new careers and relationships, find their bond tested when a wild night of partying sets off a chain of events. Years after their initial adventures, they face fresh challenges that threaten to tear them apart.

programme.title: Bissonnet 2: Back on the Blade
programme.desc: A serial killer is prowling Bissonnet street in Houston and is targeting prostitutes. Detective Wallace and Benjamin are on the case and will stop at nothing to bring him to justice.

programme.title: Bunny and Clive
programme.desc: A down-and-out reporter gets the scoop of a lifetime when he secures an interview with the notorious crime duo that is holding him hostage.

programme.title: But You Love Him
programme.desc: High school sweethearts Kelvin and Lydia have been dating for over 10 years but Lydia's promiscuous ways will lead them down a dark path and reveal an unspoken truth that will change both of their lives.

programme.title: Caught in a Trapp
programme.desc: Drama following the journey of a teen boy who is forced into a dangerous life on the streets after the death of his parents.

programme.title: Dangerous Feelings
programme.desc: A happily married woman reconnects with an old flame when he is released from prison.

programme.title: Full Clip For a Snitch

programme.title: Glitter and Grime
programme.desc: In need of cash, four friends agree to dance at amateur night at a strip club and soon find themselves thrust into the dark world of hustling.

programme.title: His Masterpiece
programme.desc: Victoria and Richard are divorced spouses that operate an art gallery together. However, Richard's aggressive nature and ulterior motives soon come to light, leading to a dangerous jealousy within him.

programme.title: Guilty Victim

programme.title: Messy

programme.title: Money Gang Lit
programme.desc: Hakeem Benson, a down-on-his-luck videographer, is evicted and adrift. He takes a risky music video gig for Bull Dawg, a notorious kingpin. But when a respected gang member is murdered, Hakeem is falsely accused and targeted. Now, in a city on fire, he's fighting for his life and meaning amidst the chaos.

With the threat of child protective services looming, the clock is ticking to find answers and rebuild shattered lives.

programme.title: Toxic Affair

programme.desc: Hired gun Reb Kittridge (Audie Murphy) goes to work in Montana for Matt Telford (Donald Randolph), who is looking to take over Dan Saxon's (Paul Kelly) fertile cattle land. What Reb doesn't bargain for is becoming a landowner himself after winning Saxon's property in a card game. Suddenly, he must work with Saxon and daughter Rita (Susan Cabot) to run the cattle to a rail line in order to make a mortgage payment. To stop him, Telford hires Reb's former partner, Johnny Lake (Charles Drake).

programme.title: Six Black Horses
programme.desc: A widow (Joan O'Brien) crosses the desert with a drifter (Audie Murphy) and the gunman (Dan Duryea) who killed her husband.

programme.desc: A gang of outlaws is murdering miners and taking over their claims, but they cross the wrong man when they target the father of Luke Cromwell (Audie Murphy), a gunslinger known as the Silver Kid. The local marshal, Lightning Tyrone (Stephen McNally), is also tracking the gang, but receives a crippling injury during a gunfight. Tyrone deputizes Cromwell, and the two take on the murderers together, while trying to resist the charms of Opal (Faith Domergue), the new beauty in town.

Hold (Denver Pyle), who believes he should be sheriff. Left to die, Chad rallies and goes off in search of the train robbers. programme.desc: The ultimate compilation of ball busting fails.

programme.title: Bob Smiley: You're Just Hungry
programme.desc: The fast-talking comedian discusses touring with a Christian group, laughter being the best medicine, and having a last name TSA agents love.

programme.title: Bob Smiley: Tartar Terrorist
programme.desc: The clean comedian discusses his experiences in Arkansas, why he won’t make fun of the Clintons, and his weird moments at the airport.

programme.title: Chris Gethard: Half My Life
programme.desc: The beloved stand up hits the road to tour the country and discuss what he’s learned in his career, what he still loves about comedy, and more.

programme.title: Dov Davidoff: Lower Your Expectations
programme.desc: Performing in Chicago, the brash stand-up comedian discusses the nonsensical truths in our culture, and wonders what America is becoming.

programme.title: Jay Mohr: Altamont
programme.desc: Comedian Jay Mohr tackles subjects from unexpected visitors, bringing up children to working with Chris Farley on SNL in his new stand-up special.

programme.title: Jay Mohr: American Treasure
programme.desc: The popular comedian tells deeply personal stories from his fascinating life and presents his famously funny celebrity impressions and observations.

programme.title: Jim Gaffigan: Quality Time
programme.desc: The comedian discusses why we aren’t honest about not wanting to attend events, lying to children, and so much more in this hilarious special.

programme.title: Jim Gaffigan: Noble Ape
programme.desc: The stand-up comedy star describes how his wife faced a medical crisis, and he attempted to hold down the fort for their five children.

programme.title: Leanne Morgan: So Yummy
programme.desc: The stand up comic discusses dogs, cheerleading camp, the struggles of being an empty nester, and joining Weight Watchers for the 7th time.

programme.title: Sinbad: Make Me Wanna Holla
programme.desc: A stand-up special covering the everything from the life advice his parents gave him to people with no talent having clothing lines and more.

programme.title: Michael Ian Black: Very Famous
programme.desc: Michael Ian Black is very famous. Well, he’s not that famous, but he keeps telling the world he is. Filmed live in Philadelphia, Michael Ian Black’s sarcastic sense of humor comes to the stage in his latest comedy special.

programme.title: Jeff Dunham: Arguing With Myself
programme.desc: Join Jeff and his outspoken felt pals: Walter, Peanut, José Jalapeño, Bubba J, and Sweet Daddy Dee for an evening of laughs and observations.

programme.title: Jeff Dunham: All Over the Map
programme.desc: A comedy set and travelogue by comedian Jeff Dunham as he and his ventriloquist’s dummies perform globally from Iceland and London to Israel.

programme.title: Jeff Dunham: Controlled Chaos
programme.desc: The ventriloquist comedian provides fresh, hilarious material with his favorite puppets; Walter, Peanut, and two brand new characters.

programme.title: Jeff Dunham: Spark of Insanity
programme.desc: Jeff and his felt friends Walter, Melvin the Superhero Guy, Peanut, and others share their latest and greatest thoughts about our times.

programme.title: Chad Daniels: As Is
programme.desc: Recorded live at the Acme Comedy Co. in Minneapolis Chad Daniels tackles such topics as airline travel, his kids and puberty. Uncut & uncensored!

programme.title: Tom Segura: Completely Normal
programme.desc: Radio, television and live performance veteran Tom Segura shares a few off-hand appraisals on hotels and hobbies with a cool and thoughtful demeanor.

programme.title: Joey Coco Diaz: Sociably Unacceptable
programme.desc: It's been 25 years in the making and now Uncle Joey Diaz is at Seeso for his first socially unacceptable special. Prepare yourselves, savages.

programme.title: Tom Papa: Live in New York City
programme.desc: With a shiny happy exterior, an offbeat, demented interior and direction from Rob Zombie, Tom Papa’s “Live In New York City” attacks our hilarious attempts at happiness as life piles up around us. He jokes about becoming a parent, family pets, and boundaries for technology.

programme.title: Jose Velasquez Hoozay: The Salvadoran
programme.desc: From his mom’s backyard to the big stage, Jose "Hoozay" Velasquez is taking the comedy world by storm with his heartwarming, hysterical storytelling.

programme.title: SXSW Comedy with W. Kamau Bell (Pt. 1)
programme.desc: Part one of a two-part special in which W. Kamau Bell hosts performances from Matt Braunger, Mark Normand, Iliza Shlesinger, and others at SXSW.

programme.title: SXSW Comedy with W. Kamau Bell (Pt. 2)
programme.desc: Part two of a two-part special in which W. Kamau Bell hosts performances from Joe DeRosa, Rachel Feinstein, Wyatt Cenac, and others at SXSW.

programme.title: Todd Glass Talks About Stuff
programme.desc: In this caustic comedy special, veteran stand-up quipster Todd Glass makes audiences howl with his take on some of society's unspoken "rules," from complimenting kids to giving directions.

programme.title: Shayne Smith: Alligator Boys
programme.desc: This comic delivers wild stories about everything, from a failed robbery, to a wrestling match in the NYC subway, and even about saving a dog’s life.

programme.title: Shayne Smith: Prison for Wizards
programme.desc: Award-winning stand-up and former gang member Shayne Smith talks Azkaban prison tattoos, diabetic dogs, hacker moms, and more in this live special.

programme.title: Matthew Jenkins: I Also Have Merch
programme.desc: Comedian Matthew Jenkins takes on creative ways to combat racism, how getting a degree in jail is free, and helping Simba from “The Lion King.”

programme.title: Joe Piscopo: A Night at Club Piscopo
programme.desc: Joe Piscopo brings his variety of talents to Atlantic City for an intimate night of entertainment. Join him for an unforgetable night at Club Piscopo.

programme.title: Jeff Cerulli: Live At The Bomb Shelter
programme.desc: Comedian Jeff Cerulli gives his takes on helping his aging father out, not getting the true crime craze and being a dog dad at the Bomb Shelter Comedy Show in NYC.

programme.title: Dan Naturman: A Little Bit Bananas
programme.desc: Dan Naturman delivers a barrage of observational comedy about marriage, Amazon Prime, the unimpressive nature of being an uncle and life's hypocrisies.

programme.title: Keep Your Distance: Vol. 17
programme.desc: KevOnStage hosts a night of stand-up comedy featuring Tony Valentino and Brennon Edwards.

programme.title: Kevin Hart Presents: Lil Rel Howery - RELevent
programme.desc: Hart hosts the high-energy comic who riffs on his upbringing, raising kids, and public transportation, proving he can be hilarious and relevant.

programme.title: Jimmy O. Yang: Good Deal
programme.desc: The Silicon Valley star uses his debut stand-up special to discuss tall women, disappointing his parents, and how he learned English from rap videos.

programme.title: Bob Saget: That’s What I’m Talkin’ About
programme.desc: Known for being family friendly, funnyman Bob Saget transforms into a foul-mouthed explainer and raconteur in this live stage show from Seattle.

programme.title: Bob Saget: Zero to Sixty
programme.desc: The popular stand-up declares himself to be the last TV father viewers can trust, as he shares funny words of wisdom and new, original comedy so

programme.title: Craig Ferguson Presents: Hobo Fabulous
programme.desc: Craig shares his reasons for getting into comedy and the reason he is never doing another standup special.

programme.title: Tim Allen: ReWires America
programme.desc: Tim Allen grunts, he burps, he soups up his lawnmower and he's damn proud of it! Filled with family bits as well as the stuff we've come to love him for: tools, engine and cars. In this 1991 special, we see the Tim Allen we grew to love.

programme.title: Tim Allen: Men Are Pigs
programme.desc: In this special from 1990, Tim Allen proves men are pigs as he discusses family and the differences between women and men. In "Men are Pigs" , you see the groundwork for his successful show, "Home Improvement".

programme.title: Moshe Kasher: Live in Oakland
programme.desc: Back on his home turf Oakland-bred comic and best-selling author, Moshe Kasher, finds comedy in uproarious stories about the people he's met and how they see him.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Dan feels helpless when Louise refuses to let him put himself at risk for her; when Jackie learns the competing restaurants are posting negative reviews.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Becky meets up with an old friend and feels pressured to impress him; Dan questions his position at the hardware store, leading to a massive fight between him and Ben.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Becky is working double shifts and taking classes at the community college, resulting in less time with her daughter; Dan decides to foster an elderly dog.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Darlene learns shocking news about a friend; the Conners family comes together to help someone close to them.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Dan encounters a surprise visitor who shares some unsolicited advice, leaving him feeling guilty; Darlene welcomes a new employee to Wellman Plastics.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Mark is stressed and exhausted from studying for an important entrance exam, so Darlene steps in to help, but her plan backfires.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Darlene makes a decision about her trip to Hawaii, leading to a heated encounter with Barb; Becky gives a presentation on addiction to Mark's class.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Two proposals bring big changes to the Conner family; Jackie uses her Jeopardy infamy to her advantage.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: The Conners continue to deal with the pandemic while navigating breakups and newfound sobriety, all while planning an upcoming wedding.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: After Ben declines Darlene's proposal, she starts to ponder why she tends to be so negative in life, prompting her to start a journey of self-discovery, which leads to Pastor Phil.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Part of Becky's recovery program mandates she make amends with people she has lied to over the years, leading her to meet up again with a former high school friend.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: It's Dan and Louise's wedding day, but it's a far-from-perfect, especially when a weather report forecasts that a tornado is headed for Lanford creating chaos and surprises.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: The Conners are ready for Halloween, despite a leaky roof that forces Dan and Louise to cancel their honeymoon

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Darlene worries about Mark making friends at his new school; Mark turns to Ben for advice; Becky finds herself thriving in college and starts thinking about her future plans.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Jackie devises an offbeat plan to serve the customers at The Lunch Box, it's anything but smooth; Dan begins to sell furniture to make room for his new bride and her things.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: As Louise starts to move in, Dan decides to sell the furniture he bought for Roseanne when they were married; he soon finds parting with the furniture harder than he thought.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Darlene brings Nick to the hardware store, where they run into Ben, who invites Nick to join him at The Lunch Box to watch a Bears game.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: The Conners celebrate Mark when he makes Dean's List at his new magnet school; Jackie continues to navigate her relationship with Neville.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Darlene continues to navigate her breakup with Ben; Dan offers to talk with Aldo's dad when he doesn't approve of Harris and Aldo's relationship.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Mark returns to public school and finds it hard to fit in; Darlene isn't happy when she finds out what Mark's doing to make extra cash for college.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Darlene loves the house she's looking at, but when the realtor says she has a chance of getting the house if she's part of a couple, she convinces Ben to pretend to be her partner.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: The family is locked down in their home due to a shooting incident in the neighborhood.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: When Becky outs her relationship with Professor Davis, it's his erratic behavior that sends Becky down a path to reevaluate their relationship.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Darlene searches for a home that she and Becky can afford; after an attempt to boost business at The Lunch Box goes awry.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Dan enlists a few friends to help him with the funeral home remodel; Robin and Darlene take a big career risk.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Darlene struggles to cope with the stress of construction; Lou returns as Mark's contrabassoon teacher.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Ben and Darlene are excited to share their good news with the family, but Neville and Jackie, and Aldo and Harris, share their own very similar news.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: The Conners gear up for a megawedding; Jackie & Neville, Darlene & Ben, Harris & Aldo are planning for a big day, but discover that some of the couples may not be on the same page.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: In an effort to save money, Darlene and Ben, and Jackie and Neville head off on their joint honeymoon, where their cost-saving measures contribute to a tension-filled getaway.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: When Jackie moves in with Neville, he shows up to their home with an excess of animals from his vet office; Darlene and Ben argue Darlene is turning into her father.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Mark is anxious to get his driver's license; Darlene attempts to teach Mark to drive, but her frantic energy sends him running to Ben for help; Harris meets a new guy online.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: When a relative passes away unexpectedly, the family must debate about who should gain custody of a jaded nephew, who is just shy of his 18th birthday.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Harris takes a stand on an issue that ruffles a few feathers in Darlene's new neighborhood.

programme.title: The Conners
programme.desc: Darlene receives a promotion, but she quickly learns the fancy title is not all it's cracked up to be; when Louise loses her job at Casita Bonita, an opportunity arises.

programme.desc: Jackie must come to terms with her mother becoming ill; Darlene's job hunt proves to be more difficult than anticipated.

programme.desc: The death of a beloved teacher from Dan and Jackie's high school brings old classmates together.

programme.title: Dr. G: Medical Examiner
programme.desc: A man is found dead in a motel room due to multiple shotgun wounds. Dr. G proves the scene isn't what it seems.

programme.title: Dr. G: Medical Examiner
programme.desc: A teen is eating breakfast when his dad starts stabbing himself. As the investigation unfolds, police begin to doubt the story. Dr. G must determine if this was a suicide or a murder.

programme.title: Dr. G: Medical Examiner
programme.desc: A train passenger behaves oddly en route to Arizona and then disappears. The next day his bloodied body is found in Texas. Dr. G attempts to piece together this bizarre tragedy.

programme.title: Dr. G: Medical Examiner

programme.title: Dr. G: Medical Examiner

programme.desc: From singing Pastors to Wookies, this week is jammed pack with Characters, who battle it out on the Qualifier with amusing attempts at the inconceivable Field Goal Swing. Its no easy task to earn a spot in the Wipeout Zone this week with the Dreadmill, sorting the men from the boys. Contestants must dodge the wrecking ball while shooting basketballs. Although there is one man who came prepared, Dodgeball Champion Dan Bennett, whose “Duck, Dive, Dip, Dodge and Dunk” training comes into play.

programme.title: Wipeout Australia
programme.desc: Showdowns don’t come more epic than this. Pro Wrestler Mikey takes on Wipeout Australias very own Tom Cruise. Its neck and neck through the qualifier which this week includes a real Mission Impossible, Dirty Balls. The trash talk is as fierce as the competition and continues to the Wipeout Zone with the winner taking not only the $20,000 but also the glory. Australia is also introduced to a new kind of “violent love”, the Fight Hug.

programme.desc: Fashion comes to Wipeout Australia as we meet Adam “God’s Gift” Marshall, a self styled Ad man who conquers the Qualifier. Other Catwalk moments include the Grandpa in his Red Speedos and a Rainbow Bright Personal Ad. If jumping a revolving arm while balancing on a pedestal above water isn’t enough for you then you’ll love this weeks Sweeper. Contestants are armed with Dodgeballs, to be deployed at each other, creating horrific hits and spectacular spills. The Dizzy Dummy claims its first victim as Dummy Tummy rejects a contestant and the Wipeout Zone becomes a battle of young and old.

programme.desc: Monty Don is asked to rescue two nightmare outside spaces. In Salisbury, newly engaged Glenn and Zoe want a brand new garden and most of all they are desperate to produce flowers for their wedding. Currently their steeply sloping garden is completely unusable.

programme.title: Big Dreams, Small Spaces
programme.desc: In Leyton, Tariq and Sairah want to turn their dumping ground of a back yard into a sumptuous award-winning garden, suitable for entertaining friends and family. Both have competitive jobs in the banking sector and are used to winning at work but when it comes to gardening, they don't know where to begin.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Charge your glasses as Joe Swift visits a `drinkable' allotment in London where much of the produce is grown to make beer, wine and cider. In the first of a five-part series about botany, Carol Klein looks at the capsules of potential that herald the start and end of most plants' lives: seeds.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In the second of her films about botany, Carol Klein goes underground to unravel the mysteries of roots and why they are so important to the health of our plants. Joe Swift meets fashion icon, Dame Zandra Rhodes, to explore how the flowers she likes to grow have influenced her designs over the years.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don is at Longmeadow with a host of seasonal jobs to keep his autumn garden looking beautiful for as long as possible, and in the third of her fascinating films about how plants work, Carol Klein investigates the science of stems.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In the fourth of her films about botany, Carol Klein explores the inner workings of leaves - how they harness the sun's energy and turn it into food. Joe Swift meets Griff Rhys Jones for a private tour of his remarkable garden and discovers what a passionate gardener he is.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In the last programme of the current series, Monty Don takes stock of his year at Longmeadow. We visit RHS Wisley to learn why autumn leaves are such a precious commodity for our gardens, and in the final instalment of her botany series, Carol Klein celebrates the glorious displays put on by flowering plants to attract pollinators.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team are in Wolverhampton to visit Bob and Maureen, who have a south-facing garden and are in desperate need of some shade. Maureen has lupus, and she comes out in bad rashes if her skin is exposed to the sun.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Two years ago, Annette moved into her house in Mansfield with her three sons. After doing up the house, she now needs help with the garden, but her £2,500 budget is all the money she has, so the pressure is on the team to deliver.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Emma and Steve have a beautiful home in Hoveringham overlooking the River Trent, but the only trouble is, the house and wonderful view are let down by a disaster of a front garden. They want to have some more privacy without losing the river view.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Corby, Northamptonshire, the team help Keri and Mark to see if their boring and featureless back garden can be turned into a relaxing and tranquil social space, built around a bar that the couple desire. With a whole garden to make over and a proper bar to build too, it is going to be a difficult challenge to complete.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Sue lost her husband seven years ago, and later lost her mother, making it a difficult and painful period in her life. She has finally settled down in a home in Wallasey that she feels she can start a new life in, and needs a new garden.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Retirees Kate and Derek have downsized to a property in Hagley that works for them in all ways, except for the garden. There are good bones to the space, but the old grey slab patio with its two-foot drop into a gravel seating area is a trip hazard.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: 82-year-old Anita needs her late husband's much-loved Fallowfield garden revitalised by the team. She would love the garden to be as good as it used to be, and to enjoy her daily evening gin and tonic in the sunshine.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: A single mum of two in Redditch, Holly's garden has turned into a virtual jungle. Can the intrepid threesome come up with a plan to give this family a safe and fun place to play?

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich start with a clean slate in Rutland, and come to the aid of Manjinder. She wants to create an interactive and colourful space to pass her love of gardening on to her two small nephews. Can the team make it happen on her small budget?

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers travel to Andover. Animal-lover Sue would like to share her garden with the hedgehogs who live in the neighbourhood, so which designer will create a space both safe and beautiful?

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: With two little ones and a home renovation going on, Cardiff couple Nadine and Steve have paid little attention to their spacious garden. Can Charlie and the boys come up with design ideas that blend entertaining spaces and room for growing children?

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As the growing season picks up a pace, Monty is sowing vegetables and planting out the scented plants, which he brought back from the Malvern Spring Festival. Carol is delving into the hedgerows and rooting at the bases of walls, fences and trees as she begins her journey to investigate why plants thrive in challenging conditions. We also return to Sissinghurst to find out about the changes Troy Scott-Smith is making to the world famous white garden.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow Monty's focus is on summer when he plants up containers for colour in the Jewel Garden. He also turns his attention to overcrowded ornamental grasses which, now they are beginning to put on growth, need to be split and replanted. Continuing her search for finding the right plant for the right place, Carol takes a close look at why plants survive in the cracks and crevices of walls and gives her recommendations for garden plants that will thrive in similar conditions.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: This week we are celebrating the work of the army of volunteers, which keeps gardens up and down the country looking their best for visitors. Frances Tophill continues her vegetable trials at RHS Rosemoor in Devon, when she plants out her allotment with the help of RHS volunteers and we visit the Bodnant Garden in North Wales to find out how the volunteers there guide visitors through the world famous Laburnum Arch.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: British summer time means that, from Saturday, we all have an extra hour to get out in our gardens in the evenings just as the early spring display is coming into its own. At Longmeadow, Monty Don introduces us to the coppice, a woodland area where his favourite flower, the primrose, is in bloom. As well as enjoying the seasonal colours of spring, it's time to plan the summer display. Monty sows sweet peas.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don shows what to do now to maximise next year's soft fruit crop by pruning his summer raspberries and planting on the strawberry runners he potted up a few weeks ago. As Monty harvests September's abundance in the vegetable garden he makes notes of the tastiest varieties he has grown this year which he would grow again next year, as well as sowing some quick growing crops to harvest in the autumn.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don celebrates the arrival of summer by making a start on his new scented border, and offers a few suggestions for what to be getting on with this weekend.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With one of the driest summers in many years, Monty responds to viewers' questions and has plenty of advice on how to deal with drought conditions for vegetables, fruit, flowers and containers. Carol Klein celebrates one of the most flamboyant summer shrubs, the hydrangea.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don reveals plans for a new raised seating area, surrounded by wild flowers. Carol looks at the blossom at Battsford Arboretum in the Cotswolds and now the risk of frosts should have passed, Monty plants beans and courgettes in the vegetable garden.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don demonstrates how to prepare for spring and summer with timely tasks in his damp garden and around the pond, and Joe Swift continues his series of master classes on planting design by sharing tips on how to create a tropical green oasis. Elsewhere, Carol Klein plants a viburnum that will give spring colour and fill her Devon garden with fragrance.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don visits the iconic garden of impressionist painter Claude Monet at Giverny. As well as revelling in the beauty and colour of Monet's paradise at this time of year, he has a privileged chat to Englishman James Priest, who, just this week, took up his role as head gardener of this world-renowned garden. Back at Longmeadow Monty shares his top tips for growing key culinary herbs.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Join Adam Frost in his garden, where he will be giving tips on laying turf, planting succulents and adding perennial plants to his kitchen garden to grow as cut flowers. Joe Swift travels to Marwood Hill Gardens in Devon to explore plants that thrive in moist conditions.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don makes a start on his new wild flower garden at Longmeadow and at last gets to taste the first new potatoes of the year. He also demonstrates how a radical chop of some border plants now can rejuvenate them for later in the season. Carol Klein celebrates a cottage garden favourite, the foxglove, and Joe Swift drops in on world renowned garden designer, Dan Pearson.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty presents a masterclass on growing Mediterranean herbs, plants lily bulbs for summer scent, and in the vegetable garden, he begins to sow seeds for salad root crops. Carol Klein is in Devon, where she takes a close look at late summer planting combinations. Meanwhile, in Cumbria, there's a gardener whose passion for exotic plants and dahlias has taken over his family garden and spilled over onto his allotment.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and Frances Tophill visit Wakehurst, Kew's wild botanic garden in Sussex. They not only explore the gardens but also meet the gardeners, joining in on their planting projects and finding out more about the UK's largest conservation project, the Millenium Seed Bank.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don is busy ensuring that his plants are making the most of the warmth and light, and puts to use the homemade fertilizer he made a few weeks ago. He also catches up with his tomato growing trial to see how the different techniques are performing. Carol Klein gets to grips with flowering dogwoods, perhaps the most aristocratic and elegant of all garden trees, and there's a visit to a magical topiary garden in Kent.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Carol Klein and Joe Swift are at Gardeners' World Live in Birmingham. Joe casts his expert eye over the spectacular show gardens, while Carol focuses on the delights of the floral marquee.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With the clocks going forward this weekend, there's an extra hour of gardening to enjoy. Monty makes the most of this as he begins to sow dahlia seeds for summer flowers, reveals a new project and shows what to plant now in the vegetable garden.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Carol Klein is in Hampshire with the great horticulturist, Roy Lancaster, appreciating the virtues of the often overlooked St John's wort. Joe Swift is out and about too and visits a very unusual allotment in Tottenham, North London.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With the holiday season well and truly upon us, Monty shares some timely tips on how to make sure your garden doesn't suffer while you are away. But there are some gardeners that daren't leave home, as Joe Swift discovered when he met Phillip Vowles.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With the season well underway, there's plenty of work to be getting on with in the garden at Longmeadow and there's plenty to enjoy too. Monty Don will be undertaking some timely tasks to make sure the garden remains beautiful and productive all summer long. Joe Swift visits a garden transported, lock, stock and barrel to Britain from Japan around a hundred years ago.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: The first big gardening weekend of the year gets underway with Monty dividing perennials and giving plenty of tips for how we can kick-start the garden for the season ahead. As millions of bedding plants are poised for planting in our pots and borders, Joe Swift visits a major grower to find out how they are produced and what drives the demand.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty revels in the sweet peas he grew from seed earlier in the year and shows how to keep their display going on into the weeks ahead. Meanwhile, Rachel meets a couple from Hampshire who have created their entire garden around their obsession for one plant, the hosta. Elsewhere, Carol travels to Wales to find out how one man tackled his sloping plot to make a garden on five different levels.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Carol Klein continues her journey across the nation in search of amazing plant combinations. This week, she's at Logan Botanic Garden near Stranraer famed for its colourful, exotic borders. Joe Swift drops in on a street in Bristol as the residents prepare to be judged by RHS Britain in Bloom.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: A celebration of the snowdrop, with a visit one of the world's largest collections, lovingly nurtured in a suburban back garden. Plus, Monty gives tips on what to do once they have finished flowering. Nick Bailey revels in the beauty of magnolias.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: It's the Easter weekend, which means extra time to get out and enjoy the garden. At Longmeadow, Monty plants out sweet peas for summer colour, gives advice on what to use for mulching borders and has plenty of ideas for jobs to get on with this long weekend. Carol Klein is at RHS Rosemoor to revel in the floral spring sensations that epitomise the season. In Scotland, JJ Chalmers explains how important his local park was in helping both his physical and mental rehabilitation.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As spring gets well underway, Monty Don adds to his collection of fritillaries at Longmeadow with pot-grown plants, as well as sowing some from seed. He also recommends other wildflowers which would thrive in the garden. Carol Klein meets Matt Bishop, who explains why he is attracted to later flowering varieties of daffodils and Rachel de Thame shows a viewer how to get to grips with pruning an overgrown shrubbery.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Whether there is a heatwave or a downpour, Monty has plenty of jobs to be getting on with from pruning, propagating or planting pots. Adam Frost meets Prince Charles in his garden at Highgrove to talk about the issue of biosecurity, an issue which is of great concern to him and which could have a big effect on our gardens and landscape. Adam also talks to The Prince's Head Forester to find out what gardeners can do to help.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost adds tall plants for shady conditions to his borders, demonstrates how to make a window box for growing vegetables and herbs, and gives his tips on how to grow plants successfully in peat-free compost.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty plants out some tender plants, takes cuttings from herbs and sows carrots. From her garden in Devon, Carol Klein goes back to the basics of propagating your own plants when she shows how to take cuttings.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty turns his attention to containers, planting them up for drama and summer colour in the jewel garden and showing us how to repot citrus plants. In Hampshire, Joe Swift meets the designer of an award-winning contemporary garden to explore the techniques and the plants she has used to seamlessly integrate both house and garden.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow Monty's focus is on summer when he plants up containers for colour in the Jewel Garden. He also turns his attention to overcrowded ornamental grasses which, now they are beginning to put on growth, need to be split and replanted. Continuing her search for finding the right plant for the right place, Carol takes a close look at why plants survive in the cracks and crevices of walls and gives her recommendations for garden plants that will thrive in similar conditions.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Carol Klein is with Billy Carruthers in West Lothian, enjoying his wonderful and varied collection of flamboyant peonies. Joe Swift visits world-renowned opera venue Glyndebourne in Sussex and is given an exclusive tour of their gardens.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: British summer time means that, from Saturday, we all have an extra hour to get out in our gardens in the evenings just as the early spring display is coming into its own. At Longmeadow, Monty Don introduces us to the coppice, a woodland area where his favourite flower, the primrose, is in bloom. As well as enjoying the seasonal colours of spring, it's time to plan the summer display. Monty sows sweet peas.

As Monty harvests September's abundance in the vegetable garden he makes notes of the tastiest varieties he has grown this year which he would grow again next year, as well as sowing some quick growing crops to harvest in the autumn.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: The 2024 season of Spring Thunder is officially underway. David Blanton kicks off the season in southern Florida by hunting the beautiful Williams Farms with Southern Outdoors Outfitters. The action is hot and it doesn’t take long to get on some angry longbeards.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: Kevin and Keelan Harvick head to Realtree Farms in Georgia with Bill Jordan and Roger Culpepper for their annual turkey hunt. The father and son love to spend time together in the outdoors and this trip was definitely one for the books.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: In a never-before-seen turkey hunt from 2022, Phillip Culpepper hunts with good friends in Maryland. Also, David Blanton gives us a look at what is in his turkey vest for the 2023 turkey season.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: The bond between and parent and child can grow in many ways, but nothing compares to the bond shared when hunting. The annual Father-Son hunt at FE Hill Outfitters has becoming a tradition for Heath and his son Gavin and in 2021, Gavin was able to take his largest buck to date, with his dad Heath behind the camera.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: Jennifer Brooks has spent her life answering the call to serve. From flying Blackhawk helicopters on multiple combat missions during two tours in Iraq to now serving as an Air Medical Pilot and first responder, her story is one of courage, sacrifice, and humility. In this episode of Storytellers, we join Jennifer in the field for a hunt that offers a rare moment to slow down, reflect, and honor a life dedicated to protecting others.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: Every year, hunters from across the country flock to various parts of Texas to extend their hunting season. This year, our friends from Struttinbuck did just the same. Matt and Clede make the long drive to Del Rio to hunt South Texas bucks right on the Mexico border.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: This spring, our crew was able to dive into some turkey hunting at the G2 ranch with long time TTHA Members Mike Reeber and Dan Marcasol. After a slow start, the crew was finally able to connect on a great Rio Tom and a few hogs. Learning and sharing the stories of TTHA members is something we strive to do each year.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Randy is getting the last food plot of the season in down at the Dirty Bird in Oklahoma. Then opening day of the 2025 bow season starts off in an unusual way for the crew. Randy smokes a Hog and Nate Has a really close call with one of the target bucks.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: We love our members. We spent some time in camp with Ron Chandler. Ron Recently renewed his TTHA Membership in 2022 and was awarded a member hunt for 2023. We spend time reflecting on what hunting means to Ron and spending time in camp at G2 with hunters alike.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Nate catches up with the Oklahoma buck that he had the close call with, and Cody is able to close the chapter with a buck he has been after for a few seasons.

programme.title: Storytellers
programme.desc: It’s that time again—when the boys from StruttinBuck load up and head out of Arkansas, bound for Fairfield, Texas and the FE Hill Ranch. Another year, another hunt, and another chapter in a tradition that’s grown into something far bigger than the hunt itself. This annual father-son trip is built on early mornings, shared stories, and moments that stick with you long after the season ends. It’s the memories made over the years that keep calling them back—and this year will be no different.

programme.title: American Heroes Outdoors
programme.desc: On this episode, we travel to Missouri River near Chamberlain, SD and connect with the South Dakota chapter of Paralyzed Veterans of America for the Joel Niemeyer "Gone Fishing" event. We get to guide and share the story of 100 year old WW2 Veteran Kenneth Carlson and his family. We also honor our next Rochester, MN Scheels Hometown Hero, Dustin Draeger!

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: The boys are at the Dirty Bird in Oklahoma getting the Oklahoma turkey season kicked off.

programme.title: The Raven Project
programme.desc: Bow season is finally here and the boys are at the Dirty Bird in Oklahoma. Randy is chasing a buck he named "The Camp Buck" because of how close the buck is to camp. If Randy can get it done this will be his biggest buck to date in Oklahoma.

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: Spring Thunder continues to roll across New York State turkey hunting public land gobblers!! After using OnX to find a large tract of public land along the shores of an Adirondack Mountain lake, the guys load up the boat to try and strike up a gobbling turkey.

programme.desc: David Blanton has a gobbler in his lap right off the Roost in Kansas hunting with JB Outfitters.

programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh
programme.desc: In this episode Andy and his friend Scotty take on Kodiak Island, Alaska for an adventure of Salmon fishing and hunting black tail deer. Whilst traversing steep mountains in the company of Kodiak brown bears and taking on cold Alaskan waters, Andy presents useful information for fellow outdoor enthusiasts all whilst building friendships with the great people of Kodiak Big Timber Lodge.

programme.title: Into The Blue
programme.desc: Captain Scott Walker heads swordfishing out of Hawks Cay with his friend, retired WWE wrestler Travis Tomko.

programme.desc: Captains Scott Walker and Ariel Medero fish with David Moss from the Nature Conservancy out of Hawks Cay sampling the bottom fishing and deep dropping. In between the action they learn about the Nature Conservancy’s mission to promote sustainable and transparent fishing practices.

programme.title: Into The Blue

programme.title: Spring Thunder
programme.desc: Tyler and Colton Jordan go after the eastern bird in the quest for Colton's first ever Grand Slam. David Blanton also hunts opening day and has 6 gobblers come into the decoy.

programme.title: The Journey Within
programme.desc: Join Mark Peterson on an epic Arctic Grizzly hunting expedition in the unexpectedly warm Alaskan wilderness. Spot and stalk alongside Mark as he navigates the icy landscapes using a boat for transportation, all while tracking down this legendary predator. Experience the thrill of the chase and the breathtaking beauty of the Arctic scenery in this adrenaline-fueled adventure set in the heart of Alaska.

programme.title: Upland Slam
programme.desc: In Part 3, Day 3, Mark Peterson wraps up his time in Wyoming upland hunting with an epic partridge hunt. Does he run into some chukars along the way? Take a look inside this episode to find out!

programme.title: Chasin' The Sun
programme.desc: When the Bay Point Billfish Open returns, we hop on board the Ellie B for some offshore competition and follow the event’s epic dockside party.

programme.title: Chasin' The Sun
programme.desc: Capt Justin & Meredith’s beautiful Panama City Beach morning is met with a slack tide so a new path to redfish success is found under a popular bridge.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Over a decade after the game changing Longbeard XR was introduced by Winchester, the wait is finally over for their next ground breaking turkey load. After years of perfecting the recipe behind the scenes, Winchester finally unveiled the new Longbeard TSS at a turkey camp in northern Michigan. First watch the jaw dropping Longbeard TSS pattern board testing, then see it go to work on some spring gobblers.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Nestled in the hills of Ohio just west of the Appalachians is a very special deer camp ripe with memories from deer season’s past. With the development of the 350 Legend straight-walled cartridge by Winchester in 2019, a world of opportunities now existed in states like Ohio and Michigan. In this episode, with the 350 Legend in hand, three mature whitetails meet their maker, from the same blind, over the course of three seasons.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Nestled in the hills of Ohio just west of the Appalachians is a very special deer camp ripe with memories from deer season’s past. programme.title: Behind The Draw
programme.desc: Shawn finally connects with a big Montana Public Land Mule Deer after many incredible stalks throughout the week. This week was one for the books filled with many ups and downs! We can't thank our buddy Cody enough for inviting us out! Don't miss Part 2 of this epic Montana Bow hunt!

programme.title: HUnting Land Man
programme.desc: On this episode of Hunting Land Man Season 3, Slade and the crew head north to scout out, plan, and put in the plots to lay the groundwork for this falls hunting season in the Midwest!

programme.title: Shoshone Adventures
programme.desc: No roads. No shortcuts. This was a true backcountry elk hunt in the deep New Mexico wilderness—packed in on horseback, living out of camp, and chasing bugling bulls from daylight to dark. With bows in hand and rutting elk all around us, nearly every day brought close encounters, intense calling sequences, and heart-pounding moments at full draw.

programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh
programme.desc: In this episode Andy and his friend Scotty take on Kodiak Island, Alaska for an adventure of Salmon fishing and hunting black tail deer. programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh
programme.desc: In this episode Andy goes back to his home farm in Alabama for deer and quail hunting.
